home.intro:
Welcome to the optimization dashboard of the "Offene Wärmespeicherplanung" (OWP) project of the Inno!Nord initiative.

This dashboard can be used to simulate multivalent heat supply systems and the supply units used within them. You can choose from a range of unit types to build your individual heat system. These units are then parameterized, the energy-economic and policy framework conditions are defined, and a combined design and dispatch optimization is performed. The method used is mixed-integer linear optimization (MILP).

### Key Features

- Combined design and dispatch optimization based on [oemof.solph](https://github.com/oemof/oemof-solph)
- Parameterization and results visualization using a [Streamlit](https://github.com/streamlit/streamlit) dashboard
- Wide selection of typical heat supply units
- Extensive database of load data, price time series and emission factors
home.used_software_text:
#### Software used:

The open-source software oemof.solph is used for model creation and simulations. In addition, a number of other Python packages are used for data processing, preparation and visualization.

---

#### oemof.solph:

oemof.solph, as part of the Open Energy Modelling Framework, is a powerful simulation tool for energy systems. The package makes it possible to optimize unit dispatch and design their capacities. The component-based structure combined with generic unit classes provides a high degree of flexibility with regard to system topology and parameterization. Further information on oemof.solph can be found in its [online documentation](https://oemof-solph.readthedocs.io).

#### Additional packages:

- [Streamlit](https://docs.streamlit.io) (graphical user interface)
- [NumPy](https://numpy.org) (data processing)
- [pandas](https://pandas.pydata.org) (data processing)
- [Matplotlib](https://matplotlib.org) (data visualization)
home.publications_text:
[1] Malte Fritz, Jonas Freißmann & Ilja Tuschy. Open Source Web Dashboard zur Unterstützung der Konzeption von Wärmeversorgungssystemen. Tagungsband zur 3. Konferenz der Norddeutschen Wärmeforschung, S. 31-36. Hochschule Bremen, 2026. [doi:10.26092/elib/5016](https://doi.org/10.26092/elib/5016).

[2] Jonas Freißmann, Malte Fritz & Ilja Tuschy. Hochtemperaturwärmepumpen in der Nah- und Fernwärmeversorgung - Technologieperspektive für den kurz- und mittelfristigen Einsatz in multivalenten Systemen. Abschlussbericht, Zentrum für nachhaltige Energiesysteme, 2024. [doi:10.5281/zenodo.13311533](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.13311533).
home.disclaimer_text:
#### Simulation results:

Numerical simulations are calculations using suitable iterative methods based on the specified boundary conditions and parameters. In individual cases, not all possible influences can be taken into account, so deviations from practical experience may occur and should be considered when evaluating the results. Accordingly, all information and results are provided without warranty.

energy_system.heat.description: <small>*This tab defines the data for heat supply. Real generation data (heat load and losses) are available for the cities of Flensburg and Sønderborg. Alternatively, custom hourly time series can be used. For the available datasets, an exact period can be selected. In addition, the data can be scaled to adapt them to individual use cases.*</small>
energy_system.network.description: <small>*This tab defines how heat network costs are considered. You can choose between specific network costs, total network costs or no consideration of network costs. For specific network costs, the route length and specific costs must be specified.*</small>
energy_system.system.description: <small>*This tab is used to select the heat supply units and their number (field next to the respective image). These units are then parameterized in the Units tab.*</small>
energy_system.units.description: <small>*This tab defines the economic and technical parameters of the previously selected heat supply units. The stored values are based on literature data and should be adjusted for the respective simulation if necessary. For each unit, it can also be decided whether the capacity should be optimized. If this option is activated, the economically optimal unit size is determined in addition to optimal operation. If the toggle remains deactivated, the capacity must be specified and only the optimal dispatch of the unit is calculated.*</small>
energy_system.supply.description: <small>*Depending on the previous selections, this tab defines all required data for the respective fuels as well as solar irradiation.*</small>
energy_system.misc.description: <small>*This tab defines other parameters. These include economic and optimization-specific parameters. For the duration of the optimization, the choice of solver and MIP gap are decisive. It is also possible to limit the maximum simulation duration.*</small>
energy_system.supply.solar.description: <small>*You can choose between three predefined locations (North: Schleswig, Central: Chemnitz, South: Stuttgart) and custom datasets. For the available datasets, an exact period can be selected and the data can be scaled if necessary to adapt them to individual use cases. Note: The length of the solar irradiation time series must match the length of the heat load time series.*</small>
energy_system.supply.electricity.descrip: <small>*Both electricity time series and associated emission factors must be defined. For both quantities, variable, constant or custom datasets can be selected. For the predefined data, an exact period can be selected and the time series can be scaled if necessary to adapt them to individual use cases. In addition, the consideration of additional electricity price components can be activated. Note: The length of the electricity price and emission factor time series must match the length of the heat load time series.*</small>
energy_system.supply.gas.description: <small>*Gas price data as well as the associated emission factor and CO₂ price data must be defined. For all quantities, variable, constant or custom datasets can be selected. For the predefined data, an exact period can be selected and the data can be scaled if necessary to adapt them to individual use cases. When selecting the fuel, values for natural gas are stored as defaults, but these can be adjusted accordingly, e.g. for biogas. Note: The length of the gas and CO₂ price time series must match the length of the heat load time series.*</small>
energy_system.heat.error.negative_heat_l: Scaling results in a negative heat load. Please adjust the offset.
energy_system.network.no_costs.warning:
No network costs are considered in the optimization. This means that investment, variable and fixed operating costs for the network connection or for the infrastructure for transporting and distributing heat will not be included in the calculation.

Therefore, the reported heat generation costs represent only the costs of generation within the considered system. If additional network costs were included, the heat generation costs would increase accordingly.
energy_system.error.timeseries_length_mi: The number of time steps in the heat load data ({nr_steps_hl}) does not match those of the {series_name} ({nr_steps_other}). Please align the data.
energy_system.misc.solver.label: Solver
energy_system.misc.solver.highs_unavaila: The `HiGHS` solver is not available on this system. Please use another solver.
energy_system.misc.solver.unavailable: The `{solver}` solver is not available on this system. Please use another solver.
energy_system.error.insufficient_heat_ca:
The selected heat units are not sufficient to cover the maximum heat demand. Possible approaches to solving the problem could be:

- Increase the installed or maximum installable capacity of the units

- Add other units or increase the number of existing units

- Add a thermal storage unit
energy_system.system.select_units.label: Select the heat supply units that can be used in the system.
energy_system.system.unit_count.label: Number of {unit}
optimization.error.no_parameters: **Error:** No parameters are available yet. You must first configure the energy system on the designated page.
optimization.overview.timeseries.subhead: Time series in the heat supply system
optimization.overview.parameters.subhead: Parameters in the heat supply system
optimization.error.infeasible:
The optimization problem could not be solved.

The unit sizes may have been selected such that they are not able to cover the heat load in every time step and/or charge a thermal storage unit.

Possible approaches to solving the problem could be:

- Adjust the installed capacity or the limits of the capacity to be installed for the units

- Add a thermal storage unit

- Add a flexible external heat source
optimization.error.unknown_solver:
An unknown error occurred during optimization.

Check the selected parameters and try to start a new optimization.
results.error.no_results: **Error:** No results are available yet. You must first run an optimization on the designated page.
results.overview.description: <small>*The optimization results are presented in a clear overview. These include the (optimal) capacities, the resulting heat production, and the economic and ecological key figures. After the analysis, a new energy system can either be configured. If the results are to be used for further analyses, a standardized report can be downloaded, or alternatively the complete result data and parameters can be exported.*</small>
results.metric.gas_emissions_t: Emissions from gas consumption in t
results.metric.electricity_emissions_t: Emissions from electricity consumption in t
results.metric.electricity_credit_emissi: Emission credits from electricity production in t
results.unit_commitment.description: <small>*The dispatch results are shown both as ordered annual duration curves and as the actual unit dispatch over the entire analysis period. For more detailed analysis, individual units can be hidden as needed. In addition, it is possible to adjust the analysis period or aggregate the results at different levels in order to evaluate specific questions in a targeted way.*</small>
results.unit_commitment.sorted_duration_: Ordered annual duration curves of unit dispatch
results.aggregation.period.label: Select aggregation period:
results.aggregation.method.label: Select aggregation method:
results.electricity_production.descripti: <small>*The electricity production of the system is shown. In addition to key figures, grid feed-in and internal use of the generated electricity are reported. For better context, the underlying electricity price time series is also visualized. For further analyses, all displayed data can be flexibly aggregated.*</small>
results.electricity_production.feed_in.c: Electricity fed into the grid in MWh
results.storage.description: <small>*The results of the thermal storage units are shown. In addition to key figures, the storage level as well as charging (negative) and discharging (positive) are visualized over time. For deeper analysis, all data can be flexibly aggregated.*</small>
results.advanced.description: <small>*The advanced results contain information for the solver log, which is automatically generated by the respective solver in order to return optimization-specific information for the simulation.*</small>
results.advanced.scip_no_solverlogs: The SCIP solver currently does not provide solver logs.
report.title: Optimization Report: Open Heat Storage Planning
report.charts.offline.title: Charts unavailable
report.charts.offline.text: An internet connection is required to display the charts because the Vega library is loaded from an external server.

charts.axis.weekly_internal_electricity_: Weekly internally used electricity in MWh
report.template.footer: This report was generated automatically by the OWP MILP optimization software.
